flags.note: Dispute level 2: multiple significant translation options.
Dispute level 2: multiple significant translation options.
πνεῦμα — "spirit" vs. "Spirit." Capitalization is a theological decision, not a grammatical fact. Greek has no capital letters.
σάρξ — "flesh" (physical body, neutral) vs. "sinful nature" (Paul's ethical sense). Using one English word across both contexts either over- or under-interprets Paul.
